title: UK - London - Bond St - Louis Vuitton vs Yayoi Kusama 02_5009282
tags: uklondonbondstlouisvuittonvsyayoikusama025009282 louisvuitton yayoikusama fashion brand branding collaboration colour color colourful colorful newbondst bondst london shop shopwindow windowdisplay mannequin dummy design legs stockings
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd An eye-catching window display from the Bond St Louis Vuitton store. Following on from their massive promotional Yayoi Kusams event at Harrods earlier this year the flagship Bond St branch had been taken over by their collaboration with the idiosyncratic Japanese artist. Click here for more 'shopping' related shots : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157606211569085 From Wikpedia, "she worked with Marc Jacobs (who visited her studio in Japan in 2006) on a line of Louis Vuitton products, including leather goods, ready-to-wear, accessories, shoes, watches, and jewelry. The products became available in 2012 at a SoHo pop-up shop, which was decorated with Kusama's trademark tentacle-like protrusions and polka-dots. Eventually, six other pop-up shops were opened around the world. When asked about her collaboration with Marc Jacobs, Kusama replied that "his sincere attitude toward art" is the same as her own. Louis Vuitton created a second set of products in 2023." 漏 D.Godliman

Apparently the shop wil be reopening soon for anyone out there that's desperate to spend several thousand pound on a scarf....... Click here to see more of my 'alternative' London shots : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157647920607519 From Wikipedia, "Burberry is a British luxury fashion house headquartered in London, England. It currently designs and distributes ready to wear, including trench coats (for which it is most famous), leather goods, footwear, fashion accessories, eyewear, fragrances, and cosmetics. Established in 1856 by Thomas Burberry, originally focusing on the development of outdoor attire, the house has moved into the high fashion market, developing gabardine, and products made exclusively for the brand. The first London shop opened in the Haymarket, in 1891. Burberry was an independent family-controlled company until 1955, when it was reincorporated. In 2005, it completed its demerger from GUS plc, the company's former majority shareholder. The company is listed on the London Stock Exchange and is a constituent of the FTSE 100 Index. In 2015, Burberry ranked 73rd in Interbrand's Best Global Brands report, alongside Louis Vuitton and Prada. Burberry has stores in 59 countries." 漏 D.Godliman

For the first time in the department store鈥檚 history, the exterior lights have dramatically changed; thanks to a mega takeover by Dior. Sailing across the fa莽ade of the famed Brompton Road address is a twinkling ship and a vast 17-by-17-metre gingerbread iteration of the brand鈥檚 signature compass star. But the Diorification doesn鈥檛 stop there. In a first for Harrods, the French house has taken over all 44 of the store鈥檚 Christmas windows, transforming them into festive gingerbread scenes and redone much of the festive decorations in store, plus it鈥檚 opened a Dior caf茅, an exhibition and two pop-up shops selling limited edition gifts and collectors items." 漏 D.Godliman

title: 42 Brushfield Street / A Gold. French Milliner.
tags: 2023 brushfieldstreet england fujixt30ii fujifilmxt30ii london spitalfields uk xf1855mm classicchrome eastlondon agold frenchmilliner vintagefrontage shop shopfront vintage store
description: Brushfield Street, Spitalfields. London E1 Like many shops, this has had a varied past. The signage is original, however has been covered and uncovered many times. Once run by Annie Gold and her husband, they lived above the shop. Interestingly the white stone shows the parish boundary between Christ Church and Middlesex and to the right, there is an iron arrow that is a symbol for the Board of Ordinance. Thanks to Katie Wignall for the info. Further info here: lookup.london/42-brushfield-street-spitalfields-history/ Taken with my Fujifilm X-T30 II camera and a XF18-55mm lens. Classic chrome.

title: UK - London - Fortnum and Mason - Peacock facade sq_5009283
tags: uklondonfortnumandmasonpeacockfacadesq5009283 fortnummason fortnumandmason picadilly london facade peacock sculpture publicart decoration shop store departmentstore building
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd A fabulour peacock sculpture decorating the front of the famous Fortnum & Mason store on Picadilly in London. Think this was probably in aid of King Charles III's Coronation which was due two days after I took this photo. Click here for more 'shopping' themed shots : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157606211569085 From Wikipedia, "William Fortnum was a footman in the household of Queen Anne. The royal family's insistence on having new candles every night resulted in large amounts of half-used wax, which Fortnum promptly resold. Fortnum also had a side business as a grocer. He convinced his landlord, Hugh Mason, to be his associate, and they founded the first Fortnum & Mason store in Mason's small shop in St James's Market in 1707. In 1761, William Fortnum's grandson Charles went into the service of Queen Charlotte, and the connection with the royal court led to an increase in business. Fortnum & Mason claims to have invented the Scotch egg, in 1738. The store began to stock speciality items, namely ready-to-eat luxury meals such as poultry or game served in aspic jelly. During the Napoleonic Wars, the emporium supplied dried fruit, spices and other preserves to British officers and during the Victorian era it was frequently called upon to provide food for prestigious court functions. Queen Victoria sent shipments of Fortnum & Mason's concentrated beef tea to Florence Nightingale's hospitals during the Crimean War." 漏 D.Godliman

title: UK - London - Old Bond St at Christmas - Cartier 01_5007982
tags: uklondonoldbondstatchristmascartier015007982 cartier oldbondst bondst london christmas xmas shop shops england greatbritain britain unitedkingdom night shopping nightphotography red christmasshopping christmaslights
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd A few days before Christmas I spent the day in London photographing the Christmas lights and Department Store shop windows. This is the Cartier shop in Old Bond Street, reflected in one of the shiny black cars that lined the street. In the background wealthy people queue to spend tens of thousands of pounds on lumps of compressed carbon and shiny bits of metal........ Click here for more Christmas shots : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157639060428214 From Wikipedia, "Cartier International SNC, or simply Cartier, is a French high-end luxury goods conglomerate that designs, manufactures, distributes, and sells jewellery, leather goods, and watches. Founded by Louis-Fran莽ois Cartier (1819鈥?904) in Paris in 1847, the company remained under family control until 1964. The company is headquartered in Paris and is a wholly owned subsidiary of the Swiss Richemont Group. Cartier operates more than 200 stores in 125 countries, with three Temples (Historical Maisons) in London, New York, and Paris. Cartier is regarded as one of the most prestigious jewellery manufacturers. Forbes ranked Cartier on its Most Valuable Brands list as 59th in 2018 and 56th in 2020, with a brand value of $12.2 B and revenue of $6.2 B. Cartier has a long history of sales to royalty. King Edward VII referred to Cartier as "the jeweller of kings and the king of jewellers." For his coronation in 1902, Edward VII ordered 27 tiaras and issued a royal warrant to Cartier in 1904. Similar warrants soon followed from the courts of Spain, Portugal, Serbia, Russia and the House of Orl茅ans." 漏 D.Godliman

title: Jack the Chipper, Whitechapel High Street.
tags: 2022 fishandchips fujix20 fujifilmx20 london whitechapel shop store chippy fish jacktheripper jackthechipper pun name sign whitechapelhighstreet osbornstreet eastend
description: I do like the occasional fish and chips, haddock being my preference. I also like fish and chip shops as they tend not to change much. Although many are named after the owner, many go for a 'punny' name. Close by, we have 'The Perfect Plaice' and here, in Whitechapel, we have 'Jack the Chipper', conjuring up memories of Jack the Ripper. In 1888, he was a notorious murderer, who plagued the streets around Whitechapel and was never caught. Taken with my digital Fujifilm X20

title: UK - London - Old Bond St at Christmas - Cartier 01_5007982
tags: uklondonoldbondstatchristmascartier015007982 cartier oldbondst bondst london christmas xmas shop shops england greatbritain britain unitedkingdom night shopping nightphotography red christmasshopping christmaslights
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd A few days before Christmas I spent the day in London photographing the Christmas lights and Department Store shop windows. This is the Cartier shop in Old Bond Street, reflected in one of the shiny black cars that lined the street. In the background wealthy people queue to spend tens of thousands of pounds on lumps of compressed carbon and shiny bits of metal........ Click here for more Christmas shots : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157639060428214 From Wikipedia, "Cartier International SNC, or simply Cartier, is a French high-end luxury goods conglomerate that designs, manufactures, distributes, and sells jewellery, leather goods, and watches. Founded by Louis-Fran莽ois Cartier (1819鈥?904) in Paris in 1847, the company remained under family control until 1964. The company is headquartered in Paris and is a wholly owned subsidiary of the Swiss Richemont Group. Cartier operates more than 200 stores in 125 countries, with three Temples (Historical Maisons) in London, New York, and Paris. Cartier is regarded as one of the most prestigious jewellery manufacturers. Forbes ranked Cartier on its Most Valuable Brands list as 59th in 2018 and 56th in 2020, with a brand value of $12.2 B and revenue of $6.2 B. Cartier has a long history of sales to royalty. King Edward VII referred to Cartier as "the jeweller of kings and the king of jewellers." For his coronation in 1902, Edward VII ordered 27 tiaras and issued a royal warrant to Cartier in 1904. Similar warrants soon followed from the courts of Spain, Portugal, Serbia, Russia and the House of Orl茅ans." 漏 D.Godliman

title: Good as gold?
tags: london borough lbsouthwark se1 flatironsq unionst devonshirehouse shop fjroll 漏freddie
description: 60-66 Union Street, Borough, SE1 (aka Flat Iron Square) Something amiss with this short terrace of which numbers 64 & 66 are Grade II listed. Note the heavy cracks in the brickwork to the top right hand (no.60), the strange bowing of the brickwork and windows of number 64 and angled sills on number 66 (Devonshire House). Apparently serious structural defects were discovered in the 1990's. Following a period of dereliction they were in danger of collapse and they were stablised. Still the orange cup recycling bin seems happy enough. As good as gold indeed! www.londonpicturearchive.org.uk/view-item?key=VXsiUCI6eyJ... title: Ghost Sign: Frisby & Son
tags: london borough lbsouthwark se1 frisbyson shop ghostsign fjroll 漏freddie jazzfoodwine damascankitchen unionst rosecrown ayresst flatironsq
description: 69-71 Union Street, Southwark, SE1 (aka Flat Iron Square) It was reported in 1975 that Frisby & Son had been trading from this site for over 30 years as a grocers. Prior to street renumbering in the 1850/60's, in 1842 number 69 (formally number 170) was Thomas Lankford (cheesemonger). According to the London Street Directory for 1882 Jason Vousden (wholesale confectioners) was at number 71 and in 1899 William Darby (grocer) occupied number 69 and 71 was now Vousden & Tilbury (wholesale confectioners). In the later 1919 & 1921 Directories number 69 was Samuel Davison Kessick (grocer) while 71 was still occupied by Vousden & Tilbury. More recently 69-71 have housed Jazz Food & Wine, and as pictured Damascan Kitchen. www.londonpicturearchive.org.uk/view-item?i=118607&WI... _MX34641ex All Rights Reserved 漏 2022 Frederick Roll Please do not use this image without prior permission

title: [PRESERVED] London Transport 'Green Line' RMC1453 - 453 CLT
tags: london transport country green line rmc1453 aec routemaster park royal 453clt 453 clt preserved
description:
Catching the second half of the wedding party was a little more difficult, given how both buses parked up in line with each other. This unexpected find also meant losing out on a spot of a Londoner Buses heritage Routemaster... you wait for one and three come at once, yes, there's the joke. Restored to more or less as-new condition, however, RMC1453 here is numerically the first production Green Line-spec Routemaster, with a prototype under the unwieldy designation 'CRL4' (later redesignated 'RMC4') taking the cake as the first Routemaster Coach on then-London Transport's 'Green Line' network. RMC1453, though, like many ex-Green Line RMCs, was repatriated to London in the 1980s, so it spent a good deal of time in London Transport's - and latterly, Arriva London's - Central area Red livery. Not going to lie, I kind of liked it in Arriva livery, too!

The second vehicle in this wedding party that appears to be stopping at the Burger King for a meal break, preserved London Transport 'Green Line' RMC1453, a 1962 Park Royal-bodied AEC Routemaster, is seen stood on The Strand behind its NBC-liveried sister vehicle in the London Bus Company of Northfleet... er, fleet.

description:
This photograph was published online in the BBC DISCOVER WILDLIFE website in an article written by Mike Dilger titled:


'' Ring-necked parakeet guide: what they look like, what they eat and where and when to see them in the UK ''


This image had previously been selected for sale in the GETTY IMAGES COLLECTION on June 21st 2019

CREATIVE RF gty.im/1157281544  MOMENT OPEN COLLECTION and became my 3,566th frame to be selected. I now have 7,500+ images represented worldwide by them.


 


Photograph taken at an altitude of Twenty two metres, at 10:49am on Thursday June 20th 2019, in the grounds of Hyde park, a Grade 1 listed Royal Park (the largest of) of London.  Spanning an area of 350 acres, the park is divided by the Serpentine and the Long water lakes, and was created by King Henry VIII in 1536 as hunting ground. It opened to the puiblic in 1637 and was extensively improved under Queen Caroline in the early righteenth century. The Great exhibition for which the Crystal Palace was erected, designed by Joseph Paxton, was also held here.


Here, off West Carriage Drive, beside The long water within Hyde Park,  we see an adult Ring necked parakeet also known as the rose-ringed parakeet (Psittacula krameri), a medium sized parrot in the genus Psittacula of the family Psittacidae with native ranges in South Asia and Africa originally. Brought to the United kingdom and Europe as caged exhibit ornamental birds, inevitably some escaped and began breeding and in 1969 in Kent, small colonies emerged. There are now more than 8,600 breeding pairs in the UK.

Alesha Jamaican Fashion Model in Green Summer Dress D茅collet茅 Low Neckline Beautiful Cleavage Splendour in the Grass Photoshoot on Location Hyde Park London established by Henry VIII in 1536

Splendour in the Grass by William Wordsworth

What though the radiance
which was once so bright
Be now for ever taken from my sight,
Though nothing can bring back the hour
Of splendour in the grass,
of glory in the flower,
We will grieve not, rather find
Strength in what remains behind;
In the primal sympathy
Which having been must ever be;
In the soothing thoughts that spring
Out of human suffering;
In the faith that looks through death,
In years that bring the philosophic mind.

description: For our final, final photo of RATP's London operations, as well as our final photo of a TfL bus from our 2023 trip to London, we're now stuck in the queues along the Euston Road on our way to Camden. And with them in relatively short supply in central London at the time, what better way to end than with a Gemini 2? Since this photo was taken, RATP London has gone the way of the dodo, route 18 has, surprisingly, gone back to First for the first time since 2013, and this bus now works out over in White City. Again, coverage of developments with RATP and First will soon follow in our next trip! The final London bus photo from this trip into London two years prior, RATP London's VH45117, a 2013 Volvo B5LH Wright Eclipse Gemini 2 allocated to Park Royal garage (PK) and new as VH17 in old money, is seen on the Euston Road working an 18 to Sudbury.

description: Yayoi Kusama: Pumpkin - 2024 鈥淜nown for her immersive installations, large-scale sculptures and intricate paintings, Yayoi Kusama often features kabocha, or pumpkin, in her work. Since 1946 Kusama鈥檚 pumpkins have taken many forms, colours and shapes, but they are always covered in the artist鈥檚 signature polka dot pattern. Kusama鈥檚 relationship to the kabocha is rooted in her childhood 鈥?the artist鈥檚 family cultivated the plant鈥檚 seeds and their home was surrounded by fields of this squash. Pumpkins frequently appear as stand-ins for self-portraits. Kusama admires them for their everyday quality, hardiness and unique, frequently humorous forms. She has said 鈥楶umpkins have been a great comfort to me since my childhood. They speak to me of the joy of living. They are humble and amusing at the same time, and I have and always will celebrate them in my art.鈥? The artist鈥檚 sculptures have been installed in museums and galleries around the world, and various outdoor spaces, including parks, gardens and at the seaside. Since 1994, a yellow and black pumpkin is permanently on display at the Benesse Art Site Naoshima 鈥?an island in Japan鈥檚 inland sea dedicated to displaying art within nature. Pumpkin (2024) is Kusama鈥檚 tallest bronze pumpkin sculpture to date, standing at 6 metres tall and 5.5 metres in diameter. The work, installed by the Round Pond, in Kensington Gardens, offers a wide range of viewpoints and is in dialogue with the surrounding environment. Curated by Lizzie Carey-Thomas, Director of Programmes (interim) and Chief Curator Natalia Grabowska, Curator at Large, Architecture and Site-Specific Projects Production: Mike Gaughan鈥? All text above 漏The Serpentine Galleries, 2024, see: www.serpentinegalleries.org/whats-on/yayoi-kusama-pumpkin...

title: First Bus London OME46002 (39562) - YJ70 EVC
tags: first bus london ome46002 39562 optare metrodecker ev tfl westbourne park x yj70evc yj70 evc
description:
Annoyingly at this point, we had encountered one of the many quirks of London bus operation: the curtailment. The 15 bus we were riding on was caught in heavy traffic due to roadworks on Fleet Street involving my mortal nemesis, the temporary traffic light, and with the delay being too heavy, the bus pulled up at the Royal Courts of Justice and dumped us on The Strand. But it wasn't all doom and gloom, though. Recent pedestrianisation works along The Strand that have seen the segment encompassing Australia House and King's College have created a bus stand area, with buses turning on the adjacent Melbourne Place to resume services on Aldwych.

Leading a pack of about three buses in line was the 23 to Westbourne Park, which is now, ironically enough, a service operated by First Bus. Introduced in 1992 as a Routemaster service under then-LRT business unit CentreWest, the 23 went with Westbourne Park bus garage to Tower Transit when First decided to sell the garage in 2013, but eleven years later, as part of their purchase of the combined RATP Dev Transit London operation, First managed to get Westbourne Park garage back! I can only wish the best for First's running of their old garage... I wonder if there's any drivers or engineers who have been at Westbourne Park since the Centrewest days, let alone the First sell-up days.

Now ran by a company that has ran buses out of Westbourne Park two times now, First Bus London's OME46002 (39562 on paper), a 2020 Optare MetroDecker EV based from Westbourne Park garage (X), is seen stood waiting time at The Strand between runs on route 23 to Westbourne Park.

title: UK - London - Square in the square_5001408
tags: uklondonsquareinthesquare5001408 finsburycircusgardens finsburycircus londonparks park circus squareformat squares bsquare sq horizon22 22bishopsgate cityoflondon london skyscraper viewinggallery view city britain england greatbritain unitedkingdom viewpoint lookingdown uk travelphotography travelphotographer travelling travel omot nikon gb
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd A view of Finsbury Circus Gardens and the top of nearby Tower 42 (formerly both the Nat West Tower and London's tallest tower....) as photographed from the Horizon 22 viewing gallery. Although the Horizon 22 viewing gallery of 22 Bishopsgate has been open for a while, I only recently got to visit myself. Unlike the Shard, it's free, and as a result the tickets get snapped up as soon as they're released. I happened to be passing on a weekday afternoon and called in to ask if they had any 'walk up' tickets available (not that you actually have to walk up 58 flights of stairs). I had to kill about half an hour but when I returned I was up the top within minutes. P.S. I read recently that the Shard has reduced it's entry price significantly, presumably in response to a number of free viewing gallery that have opened in the last few years. More London photos here : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157601900466587 From Wikipedia, "Finsbury Circus is a park in the Coleman Street Ward of the City of London, England. The 2 acre park is the largest public open space within the City's boundaries. It is not to be confused with Finsbury Square, just north of the City, or Finsbury Park, a few miles away. The circus was created in 1812 on the site of the former Lower Moorfields, an area which was originally part of the Manor of Finsbury, a manor which had existed since the 12th century, on which the second Bethlem Royal Hospital had stood since 1675. The "circus" of the name reflects the elliptical shape of the space, similar to the circus venues of ancient Rome, in this case with a long axis lying west-east." 漏 D.Godliman

title: RATP London VH45117 - BT13 YWP
tags: ratp london vh45117 volvo b5lh wright eclipse gemini 2 tfl park royal pk bt13ywp bt13 ywp vh17
description: For our final, final photo of RATP's London operations, as well as our final photo of a TfL bus from our 2023 trip to London, we're now stuck in the queues along the Euston Road on our way to Camden. And with them in relatively short supply in central London at the time, what better way to end than with a Gemini 2? Since this photo was taken, RATP London has gone the way of the dodo, route 18 has, surprisingly, gone back to First for the first time since 2013, and this bus now works out over in White City. Again, coverage of developments with RATP and First will soon follow in our next trip! The final London bus photo from this trip into London two years prior, RATP London's VH45117, a 2013 Volvo B5LH Wright Eclipse Gemini 2 allocated to Park Royal garage (PK) and new as VH17 in old money, is seen on the Euston Road working an 18 to Sudbury.

tags: uklondonsquareinthesquare5001408 finsburycircusgardens finsburycircus londonparks park circus squareformat squares bsquare sq horizon22 22bishopsgate cityoflondon london skyscraper viewinggallery view city britain england greatbritain unitedkingdom viewpoint lookingdown uk travelphotography travelphotographer travelling travel omot nikon gb
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd A view of Finsbury Circus Gardens and the top of nearby Tower 42 (formerly both the Nat West Tower and London's tallest tower....) as photographed from the Horizon 22 viewing gallery. Although the Horizon 22 viewing gallery of 22 Bishopsgate has been open for a while, I only recently got to visit myself. Unlike the Shard, it's free, and as a result the tickets get snapped up as soon as they're released. I happened to be passing on a weekday afternoon and called in to ask if they had any 'walk up' tickets available (not that you actually have to walk up 58 flights of stairs). I had to kill about half an hour but when I returned I was up the top within minutes. P.S. I read recently that the Shard has reduced it's entry price significantly, presumably in response to a number of free viewing gallery that have opened in the last few years. More London photos here : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157601900466587 From Wikipedia, "Finsbury Circus is a park in the Coleman Street Ward of the City of London, England. The 2 acre park is the largest public open space within the City's boundaries. It is not to be confused with Finsbury Square, just north of the City, or Finsbury Park, a few miles away. The circus was created in 1812 on the site of the former Lower Moorfields, an area which was originally part of the Manor of Finsbury, a manor which had existed since the 12th century, on which the second Bethlem Royal Hospital had stood since 1675. The "circus" of the name reflects the elliptical shape of the space, similar to the circus venues of ancient Rome, in this case with a long axis lying west-east." 漏 D.Godliman

title: American Car Wash, Shoreditch
tags: american car wash shoreditch concrete park meyers brothers
description:
The now closed NCP multi-storey car park on Great Eastern Street in Shoreditch. It wore branding for Meyers Brothers of Newark, New Jersey which is still mostly intact, rusting away, as it used car lifts rather than standard ramps. A fantastic (in my opinion) concrete building, built in 1962.
The American Car Wash Co. operate below where the 'petrol garage' was, and as with many buildings in the area, is adorned with various graffiti and street art.

title: American Car Wash, Shoreditch
tags: american car wash shoreditch concrete park meyers brothers
description:
The now closed NCP multi-storey car park on Great Eastern Street in Shoreditch. It wore branding for Meyers Brothers of Newark, New Jersey which is still mostly intact, rusting away, as it used car lifts rather than standard ramps. A fantastic (in my opinion) concrete building, built in 1962.
The American Car Wash Co. operate below where the 'petrol garage' was, and as with many buildings in the area, is adorned with various graffiti and street art.

title: London Vintage Bus Hire 'London Bus Experience' RM1979 - ALD 979B
tags: london vintage bus hire rm1979 aec routemaster park royal ald979b ald 979b exlondon
description:
Also right off the bat, yet another Routemaster, but this time in generously-converted open-top form. Bit of a shame that the lamp post is blocking it up, but on a limited time trip out, what can you really do in these situations? This Routemaster in particular is wrapped in a livery advertising both the London Bus Experience sightseeing tour as well as 'The View from the Shard', which as of January 2025, costs a whopping 拢32 per person to do. Travelling with another, that would've brought us to 拢64. Yeah, no thanks.

The last genuine Routemaster of the trip, London Vintage Bus Hire's open-topper RM1979, a 1964 Park Royal-bodied AEC Routemaster new to London Transport which was, thankfully, given a fresh coat of paint for the 2024 season, is seen parked along the Embankment hawking the London Bus Experience.
